message.content: ["Self-attention's pairwise token interactions scale quadratically with sequence length, making it the most expensive part of the Transformer at long context lengths.", "Standard dense attention applies the same set of attention heads to every token regardless of token difficulty or information content, wasting compute.", "We propose Grouped Query Experts (GQE), a mixture-of-experts layer on top of grouped-query attention (GQA), where a router selects k query-head experts per token within each GQA group while all key-value (KV) heads remain dense and unchanged.", "On a fixed 30B token budget at the 250M parameter scale, GQE matches the all-active GQA baseline in downstream accuracy while activating half the query heads per token."]
message.reasoning_content:
We need to output a JSON array of 2-4 short strings, each a self-contained facet from the given text. No invention, no overlap. The text describes "Grouped Query Experts: Mixture-of-Experts on GQA Self-Attention." Let's break it into facets. The text says:

- Self-attention is central, expensive due to quadratic scaling.
- Standard dense attention applies same heads to every token, wasting compute.
- They propose GQE, a MoE layer on top of GQA.
- Within each GQA group, router selects k query-head experts per token, KV heads remain dense/unchanged.
- GQE keeps KV cache benefits and reduces active query-head computation.
- On a fixed 30B token budget at 250M parameter scale, GQE matches all-active GQA baseline in downstream accuracy while activating half the query heads per token.

So facets could be: (1) Problem statement: self-attention is expensive and wastes compute with uniform heads. (2) The proposed method: GQE on GQA, selecting query-head experts per token, KV heads unchanged. (3) Benefit: preserves KV cache, reduces query-head computation. (4) Experimental result: matches baseline accuracy with half query heads active. These are distinct aspects. Might combine some: facet 1: The inefficiency of standard dense attention; facet 2: The GQE architecture; facet 3: Performance parity with reduced compute. That yields 3 facets. 

Let's craft strings: Use text's own terms. "Self-attention scales quadratically with sequence length and applies the same attention heads uniformly, wasting compute." But that's two ideas: cost and uniformity. Better: separate. One facet: "Self-attention's pairwise token interactions scale quadratically with sequence length, making it expensive." Another: "Standard dense attention applies the same set of attention heads to every token, wasting compute." However, they are related. The text's main points: problem, method, outcome. So I'll output 3 facets: one about the problem (inefficiency), one about the method (GQE on GQA with router), one about the result (matching accuracy with half query heads). Ensure self-contained. Use text's phrasing: "We propose Grouped Query Experts (GQE), a mixture-of-experts layer on top of grouped-query attention (GQA). Within each GQA group, a router selects k query-head experts per token while all key-value (KV) heads remain dense and unchanged." That's method. Then result: "On a fixed 30B token budget at the 250M parameter scale, GQE matches the all-active GQA baseline in downstream accuracy while activating half the query heads per token." And problem: "Standard dense attention also applies the same set of attention heads to every token regardless of token difficulty or information content, wasting compute." That is clear. Might also include the quadratic scaling, but the wasting compute line includes that context. But the quadratic scaling is mentioned as a general statement. I could have: "Self-attention is the most expensive part of the Transformer at long context lengths because its pairwise token interactions scale quadratically with sequence length." That's one facet. Then the wasteful uniform activation is another. However, the instruction says "split a short research text into its distinct FACETS - the separate aspects, conditions or sub-claims it actually contains" and output 2-4 short strings. So I can have 4 facets: Facet 1: Self-attention's quadratic scaling cost; Facet 2: Uniform attention heads waste compute; Facet 3: GQE method with router selecting experts; Facet 4: Matching accuracy at half query-head activation. But facet 1 and 2 are related but distinct: one is about cost, the other about uniform allocation. They are separate sub-claims. I'll include both. The text says: "Self-attention is central to Transformer performance and is often the most expensive part of the Transformer at long context lengths because its pairwise token interactions scale quadratically with sequence length. Standard dense attention also applies the same set of attention heads to every token regardless of token difficulty or information content. This uniform activation can waste compute, especially as sequences grow longer and attention cost increases rapidly." So I'll extract: "Self-attention's pairwise token interactions scale quadratically with sequence length, making it the most expensive part of the Transformer at long context lengths." That's one. "Standard dense attention applies the same set of attention heads to every token regardless of token difficulty, wasting compute." That's the second. Then method: "We propose GQE, a mixture-of-experts layer on GQA, where a router selects k query-head experts per token within each GQA group, while all KV heads remain dense." Then result: "On a fixed 30B token budget at 250M parameters, GQE matches the all-active GQA baseline in downstream accuracy while activating half the query heads per token." That's four facets. But the result includes the budget and parameter scale. It's one sub-claim. So I'll output JSON array of these four strings. Ensure they are self-contained, short. No invention. No overlap: the method and result don't overlap. The problem facets don't overlap with method. So OK. I'll output exactly that.
